response.justification: Over 15 years of progressive full-stack experience with a strong match on the primary stack — TypeScript, Node.js, React, and MongoDB — combined with explicit AI-agent and MCP tooling work at the current role directly satisfies a differentiating requirement most candidates will lack. The main gaps are absence of Nest.js and Kotlin references, and no explicit Vitest or Playwright mention, but the depth of testing culture (TDD practice, test framework authorship, testing workshops) and the breadth of backend and frontend delivery make those gaps low-risk.
strengths: Current role explicitly covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling — a rare and directly required skill
Deep TypeScript/Node.js/React stack across multiple production-grade roles, with AWS and MongoDB experience
Strong testing culture demonstrated through TDD practice, open-source test framework authorship, and community workshops
concerns: No mention of Nest.js anywhere in the resume — the primary backend framework for the role
Kotlin is absent; Java appears only in older embedded/robotics context, not in web service development
No explicit reference to Vitest or Playwright, and security/auth practices are not called out directly

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP tooling experience
Core stack alignment (TS, Node, React, MongoDB, AWS)
